Deng Jin
Home Projects About
FURNIPAR
家具派

A service platform that reduces the waste of used furniture resources — enabling second-hand furniture to quickly find new owners, completing delivery and after-sales, and promoting circulation and recycling.

致力于减少二手家具资源浪费——帮助二手家具快速找到新主人，促进二手家具循环利用与废旧家具回收，实现节约资源的目的。

Type
Service Design / System Design
Duration
2019.10 — 2020.6
Role
Research · Service Strategy · Concept Development · Prototyping
Scope
Used Furniture · Circular Service · Community Infrastructure
Overview / 项目概述
FURNIPAR
家具派
给旧家具第二次生命
Giving Second-Hand Furniture a Second Life

Furnipar is a service platform dedicated to the circular economy of second-hand furniture — integrating products, services, spaces, people, communication, and digital tools into a seamless ecosystem. It helps second-hand furniture quickly find new owners, and provides logistics and after-sales support throughout the process.

家具派是一个专注旧家具循环经济的服务平台，将产品、服务、空间、人际、沟通与数字工具整合为无缝生态系统。帮助旧家具快速找到新主人，并提供物流与售后全程支持。

The platform promotes the circulation of second-hand furniture and the responsible disposal of waste furniture — with the overarching goal of conserving resources, reducing landfill waste, and cultivating environmental awareness in communities.

平台致力于推动旧家具流通与废弃家具的负责任处置，以节约资源、减少填埋废弃、在社区中培育环保意识为核心目标。

Service Components / 服务构成
Storage Box
社区存储箱
Community-level lifting-and-transversal boxes for damage-free bulk furniture storage
Offline Shop
线下门店
Physical store combining consignment, workshops, and DIY experience in the community
Website & Database
网站与数据库
Backend platform managing furniture IDs, inventory, recycling data, and logistics coordination
Mobile App
移动应用
User-facing app for browsing, matching, storage drop-off, DIY tutorials, and delivery tracking
"Rather than designing a single object, the goal was to organise relationships — between stakeholders, touchpoints, resources, and service flows — into a system that makes the circular economy of furniture not just possible, but intuitive."
「这个项目的目标不是设计一个单一产品，而是组织关系——将利益相关者、触点、资源与服务流整合为一个系统，让家具循环经济不仅可行，更变得直觉自然。」
Methodology / 方法论
DOUBLE
Diamond
双钻模型
The Double Diamond model divides the design process into four key phases — ensuring the right problem is solved before the right solution is found. / 双钻模型将设计流程划分为四个关键阶段，确保在找到正确解决方案之前，先解决正确的问题。
Phase 1 — Diverge / 发散
DISCOVER
发现

Stay curious. Explore the problem space through user research, market analysis, and stakeholder interviews — without assuming answers. Collect data, build empathy, understand pain points.

保持好奇，通过用户研究、市场分析与访谈探索问题空间，不预设答案。

Phase 2 — Converge / 收敛
DEFINE
定义

Distil the research into core insights. Use journey maps and affinity diagrams to synthesise findings into a clear, focused design challenge — the pivot between the two diamonds.

将研究提炼为核心洞察，通过旅程地图与亲和图形成清晰的设计挑战定义。

Phase 3 — Diverge / 发散
DEVELOP
构思

Generate as many potential solutions as possible. Run co-design workshops, brainstorming, and low-fidelity prototyping to explore directions before converging.

尽可能多地生成潜在解决方案，通过共创、头脑风暴与低保真原型探索方向。

Phase 4 — Converge / 收敛
DELIVER
交付

Test, refine, and validate the best solution. User testing → iteration → implementation. Deliver a verified, feasible final solution with feedback mechanisms built in.

测试、精炼并验证最佳方案，通过用户测试与迭代，交付可行的最终解决方案。

Discover / 发现 — Background
WHY DOES USED
FURNITURE BECOME
Waste So Easily?
旧家具为何如此容易变成废弃物？
I researched the current state of the furniture and second-hand furniture market. I found that the problem was more serious than I thought — it is not just a matter of trading. / 调研表明，旧家具问题远比预想严重，绝不只是买卖不顺畅那么简单。
Why is the waste of old furniture so important? / 旧家具废弃问题为何如此重要？
85 million tons
of waste wood every year / 每年产生8500万吨废弃木材
Wood Consuming Industry
Furniture industry 28.6% · Construction & decoration 22.9%
A large proportion of consuming / 消耗比例极大
Only 63%
reuse rate / 再利用率仅63%
Wood Supply-Demand Ratio
Huge gap in supply and demand — supply far below demand / 供需缺口巨大
111.944M m²
58% wood were imported / 58%木材依赖进口
Wood Reuse Efficiency
3m² waste wood produces only 1m² artificial board / 3m²废木仅产出1m²人造板
Why is there a huge amount of waste furniture? / 为何存在如此大量的废弃家具？
🏗️
Booming real estate market / 房地产市场繁荣
1.278 Billion

A total of 1.278 billion m² of commercial housing sold nationwide during 2009–2019.

Real estate drives furniture consumption. / 房地产驱动家具消费。

🏭
Developing furniture industry / 家具行业快速发展
2.2 Trillion

Total output of wood products reached 2.2 trillion yuan. 80,000+ wood furniture enterprises.

Industrial development promotes furniture iteration speed. / 产业发展加速家具迭代。

💰
Growth in household expenditure / 居民消费支出增长
3.5 Trillion

Retail sales of urban consumer goods totalled 3.5 trillion yuan (furniture 5.1%).

Income growth promotes furniture consumption. / 收入增长推动家具消费。

🏙️
Growth in urban population / 城市人口增长
73%

The permanent urban population is 73% — large population mobility creates high turnover.

Population mobility drives supply and demand for used furniture. / 人口流动驱动二手家具供需。

Conclusion

In the past decade, a large number of real estate developments and sales have greatly increased the demand for furniture. In the next decade, these furnitures will be replaced and a huge amount of used furniture will be discarded — these used furnitures need to be taken seriously.

Carrying out the recycling of waste furniture is also beneficial to alleviate the shortage of wood resources, reduce environmental pollution and promote circular economy.

推进废旧家具回收，有助于缓解木材资源短缺、减少环境污染，并促进循环经济发展。

My Motivation / 我的动机

After graduating, I went to a new city for my job. I had a lot of trouble buying cheap used furniture and had bad memories. I wondered: is there something I can do? After discovering service design thinking, I decided to use this method to explore the used furniture problem.

毕业后搬到新城市，购买廉价二手家具的经历留下了糟糕回忆。我想：能做些什么改变吗？偶然接触到服务设计思维后，决定用这套方法探索二手家具问题。

Discover / 发现 — Desktop Research
WHY IS FURNITURE WASTE
Management Ignored?
为何二手家具管理长期被忽视？
Using the STEEP framework to understand the big picture — Social, Technological, Economic, Environmental, Political. This is not a single recycling failure, but a structural problem caused by low public awareness, limited policy, lagging systems, and weak market incentives. / 用STEEP框架系统分析原因——这不是单一回收环节的失效，而是由公众认知不足、政策有限、处理体系滞后与市场激励缺失等结构性因素共同造成。
E
Environment / 环境
  • Waste damage is often invisible until it accumulates
  • 缺乏直观环境损害，导致政策忽视
T
Technology / 技术
  • Disposal and reuse systems lag behind technological progress
  • 处置与再利用系统的发展落后于技术进步
P
Political / 政策
  • Policy support and public infrastructure are limited
  • 政策支持与公共基础设施有限
E
Economic / 经济
  • Incentives for recycling and reuse remain weak
  • 回收与再利用的经济激励依然薄弱
S
Social / 社会
  • Public awareness and participation are still low
  • 公众意识与参与度依然较低
Discover / 发现 — Stakeholder Mapping
UNDERSTANDING THE
Actors Behind the System
理解系统背后的参与者
Used furniture disposal is not dominated by a single actor — it is shaped by users, second-hand traders, manufacturers, governments, recyclers, and communities, each with different roles, needs, and influence. / 旧家具废弃管理并非由单一角色主导，而是受到用户、二手从业者、生产者、政府、回收企业与社区等多方共同影响。
Stakeholders Chose — Narrowing it Down to Waste Management
聚焦废弃管理的利益相关者选择

I focus on waste management and chose relevant stakeholders that occupy different spaces on the waste management chain — stakeholders that have diverse motivations to change or maintain the status.

我聚焦于废弃物管理，并选取了在废弃管理链上占据不同位置的利益相关者——他们对于改变或维持现状有着各不相同的动机。

Prime Factor / 核心参与者
Professional second-hand furniture seller 专业二手家具卖家
Used furniture reuser 二手家具再利用者
Used Furniture Producer 旧家具生产者
External Factor / 外部影响者
The Government 政府
Material Recycling Company 材料回收公司
The Community 社区
Discover / 发现 — Primary Research
LISTENING TO THE
Voices of Stakeholders
聆听利益相关者的声音
Through in-depth interviews and collaborative journey mapping, I explored how different stakeholders experience selling, discarding, repairing, or reusing furniture — making visible not only practical barriers, but also emotions such as uncertainty, lack of trust, inconvenience, and low confidence in current solutions. / 通过深度访谈与协作式旅程地图，我进一步理解了不同利益相关者在出售、丢弃、修复或再利用家具过程中的真实经历——揭示了操作层面的障碍，也显现出不确定感、不信任与缺乏信心等情绪层问题。
Interview Relative Stakeholders
访谈利益相关者的恐惧与期望

I interviewed stakeholders and summed up their greatest hopes and fears regarding this issue, using actual quotes from each participant. / 我访谈了利益相关者，汇总了他们对这一问题最深切的恐惧与期望，并使用了实际引用。

😟
Fears & Concerns
Connected to the problems / 与问题相连
Hopes & Desires
Connected to the problems / 与问题相连
😊

"The recycled furniture cannot be sold, I will lose money, and the capital turnover will be under pressure."

回收来的家具卖不出去，我会亏损，资金周转会有压力。

Professional
Seller
专业卖家

"Hoping that all my recycled furniture can be sold quickly."

希望所有回收来的家具都能快速售出。

"There are many concerns about the reuse of second-hand furniture."

对二手家具的再利用存在很多顾虑。

Furniture
Reuser
再利用者

"I hope I can't be deceived, and all processes are open and transparent."

希望不会被欺骗，所有流程公开透明。

"I am worried that the process is too complicated."

担心整个流程过于繁琐复杂。

Furniture
Producer
家具生产者

"Hoping to dispose of used furniture quickly and easily."

希望能快速、轻松地处置旧家具。

"A lot of old furniture puts pressure on the environmental protection system."

大量旧家具给环保体系带来巨大压力。

The
Government
政府

"Hoping the city and residents can work together to reduce waste and increase recycling."

希望城市与居民共同减少废弃、提升回收率。

"The accumulation of used furniture in the community brings many complaints."

社区内旧家具积压带来了大量投诉。

The
Community
社区

"Hoping the government or relevant institutions can make changes and create a better environment."

希望政府或相关机构推动改变，创造更好的环境。

"Waste furniture has low added value and high recycling pressure."

废旧家具附加值低，回收压力大。

Recycling
Company
回收公司

"Hoping the government can provide financial and technical support."

希望政府能提供资金与技术支持。

Define / 定义 — Synthesis
FROM SCATTERED PAIN POINTS
TO Key Design Challenges
从分散痛点到核心设计挑战
By organising interview findings and collaborative mapping into an affinity diagram, recurring patterns emerged across the service experience — revealing that problems in used furniture disposal are not isolated, but span information access, trust, recirculation, and repair execution. / 通过亲和图对访谈与共创资料进行整理，识别出贯穿整个服务过程的共性问题——旧家具处置中的问题并非孤立出现，而是贯穿于信息获取、信任建立、翻新流转与回收执行等多个环节。
I found 8 Key Challenges / 八大核心挑战
Control掌控感

Give customers a sense of control over the second-hand furniture they will buy.

让顾客对即将购买的二手家具拥有掌控感。

Environmental Protection环保意识

Make residents have a sense of environmental protection in their disposal choices.

让居民在处置选择中具备环保意识。

Belonging归属感

Give residents a sense of belonging to the community through furniture exchange.

让居民通过家具交换对社区产生归属感。

Achievement成就感

Give residents a sense of achievement in the renovation of old things.

让居民在改造旧物中获得成就感。

Trust信任感

Give sellers and buyers a sense of trust in second-hand furniture platforms.

让买卖双方对二手家具平台建立信任感。

Security安全感

Make residents have a sense of security for second-hand furniture transactions.

让居民在二手家具交易中感到安全。

Innovation创新感

Make peddlers have a sense of innovation — elevating the second-hand trade.

让从业者拥有创新感，提升二手交易的价值。

Cost低成本

Keep the transportation and storage cost of furniture low to make transactions viable.

降低家具运输与存储成本，使交易在经济上可行。

Develop / 构思 — Concept Generation
CONCEPT
Generation
概念生成
Understanding existing logistics systems through desktop research, and drawing inspiration from personal moving experiences to identify key design opportunities. / 通过桌面研究理解现有物流系统，并从个人搬家经历中汲取灵感，识别关键设计机会。

Desktop Research

My goal was to understand what steps were already in the works and see how I can use or improve the existing system.

Theory 1 — Reverse Logistics

The most representative research results are 2 books "Reverse Logistic" and "Reverse Logistics Planning and Implementation" written by Stock in 1992. These two works systematically discuss the theoretical problems of reverse logistics in view of the reverse flow process.

Theory 2 — Physical Distribution Logistics Management

Kroon L, Vrijens G. Returnable contains: an example of reverse logistic [J]. International Journal of physical Distribution Logistics Management 1995.

Personal Real Experience

Some inspiration from my real experiences.

Experience 1　Across the province to move by a moving company

Experience 2　Use express delivery to move furniture home

WHAT I FOUND

The cost of moving furniture in a full truck is much less than the cost of expressing furniture.

Averaging out to approximately 1:12 per piece of furniture.

Many truck drivers are idle.

After they deliver the goods to their destination, they return sometimes empty, resulting in wasted resources. I will consider how to use these resources.

Expensive furniture packaging.

During express shipping, packaging accounts for a large percentage of the cost, even more than the shipping cost. I will consider ways to reduce packaging and save on shipping costs and resources.

Develop / 构思 — Co-design Workshop
CO-DESIGN
Session
共创工作坊
Inviting stakeholders to explore possible service scenarios based on prior research findings — using brainstorming, concept generation, and voting feedback to progressively narrow direction. / 邀请利益相关者基于前期研究发现共同探索可能的服务场景，通过头脑风暴、概念生成与投票反馈逐步收束方向。

The co-design session showed that the used furniture problem can be addressed from multiple angles: community recovery, trade trust, household reuse, and transport coordination. This provided a clearer basis for subsequent concept selection and deepening.

共创工作坊表明，旧家具问题可以从社区回收、交易信任、家庭再利用与运输协同等不同层面切入，为后续方案选择与概念深化提供了更清晰的依据。

6:00–6:30
Discuss collected data and existing approaches / 讨论收集到的数据与现有做法
6:30–7:00
Brainstorming — "more is better, no criticism" / 头脑风暴——"越多越好，不批评"
8:00–9:30
Scenario rehearsal with SAP Scenes toolkit — vote to narrow scope / 用SAP场景工具排练并投票收束方向
Environmental Awareness
Cultivation
环保意识培养
Curbing wasted furniture production
Waste producers pay / 废物生产者付费
Educating people to repair rather than buying new / 教育修缮而非购新
Promote Used
Furniture Trade
促进二手家具贸易
Build confidence to trade in used furniture
Establish a standardized secondary market / 建立标准化二手市场
Transparent information and communication / 透明信息沟通
Physical shop to build confidence / 实体店建立交易信心
Disposal of
Used Furniture
旧家具的处理
Using recycling methods to disposal
Wasted furniture collected / 废弃家具收集
Rational allocation of resources / 资源合理分配
Deliver / 交付 — Offering Map
FOUR SERVICE
Offerings
四大服务提供
These four offerings span the used furniture lifecycle — corresponding to recirculation, sustainable disposal, source reduction, and logistics efficiency. Each addresses a cluster of the human needs identified in research. / 四大服务提供沿旧家具生命周期展开，分别对应再流通、可持续处置、源头减量与物流效率。每项均对应研究中识别的一组人类需求。
How the offerings were derived / 服务提供的推导来源
Strategic Direction 01
Promote Used
Furniture Trade
促进旧家具流通
Deal with waste furniture already present in the market — create channels for second-hand circulation, trust and price transparency.
Strategic Direction 02
Disposal of
Used Furniture
旧家具可持续处置
Route waste furniture into resource regeneration — community storage, affordable moving/logistics, and shared capacity.
Strategic Direction 03
Environmental
Awareness
环保意识培养
Fundamentally reduce furniture purchase & replacement speed through DIY culture, community belonging, and renovation achievement.
6 Human Need Clusters from Research / 研究识别的六组人类需求
Sense of control in old furniture provided
Sense of trust in furniture platforms
Sense of affordable moving & storage
Sense of belonging to the community
Sense of environment protection
Sense of achievement in renovation
3 directions × 6 need clusters → 18+ service concepts → condensed to 4 core offerings
The offering map was generated by cross-referencing three strategic lifecycle directions with six human need clusters identified in research — producing 18+ specific service concepts. Through stakeholder co-design and concept voting, these were synthesised into four core offerings that best balance systemic impact, feasibility, and user value. 服务提供地图通过将三个战略方向与研究中识别的六组人类需求交叉对照，生成了18个以上具体服务概念。经利益相关者共创工作坊与概念投票，整合为四项核心服务提供，以实现系统影响力、可行性与用户价值的最佳平衡。
01
Community Recovery Network
社区协同回收网络 · 旧家具再流通服务
A community-based recovery and transit network — residents use a platform to find nearby collection points, choose drop-off, temporary storage, or second-hand circulation; the backend coordinates transport, sorting, and follow-up processing.

Inspired by the three-dimensional garage, a lifting-and-transversal furniture storage box enables mass storage without damage. Users: find the nearest box on app → submit furniture info → navigate or request pickup → scan QR to confirm. Recycling companies monitor storage data in real time and dispatch trucks accordingly.

灵感来自立体停车库，升降横移存储箱实现无损批量存储。用户：APP找到最近存储箱→提交家具信息→导航或预约取件→扫描二维码确认。回收公司实时监控存储数据并据此调度货车。

Temporary StorageInfrared Volume EstimationReal-time MonitoringBetter Dispatching
02
Trust-Building Through Offline Experience
基于线下体验的信任构建服务
Physical stores combined with information verification mechanisms — strengthening user trust in second-hand furniture quality, pricing, and transaction process to promote recirculation.

The core barrier in second-hand furniture trade is not price but insufficient quality judgement and transaction trust. The offline store serves as an exhibition, learning, participation, and verification node — combining consignment, workshops, and display — transforming it into a shared platform for education and trade.

二手家具交易的核心障碍并非价格，而是品质判断与交易信任不足。线下门店被定义为集展示、学习、参与与验证于一体的服务节点——结合寄售、工作坊与陈列，转化为教育与交易的共享平台。

Build Trust via PhysicalConsignment + WorkshopFurniture ID Card
03
DIY Remaking & Material Circulation
家庭再造与材料循环服务
Providing remaking tools, material support, and reuse guidance — helping old furniture be repaired, remade, or disassembled for recycling in the home context, extending its useful lifecycle.

Through DIY tutorial sharing, tool and material support, and local same-day delivery, environmental awareness is turned into participatory remaking practice. Users access knowledge online, purchase a DIY Box delivered in 30 minutes, and can bring remade furniture back into the trade system — connecting learning, making, and recirculation.

通过DIY教程分享、工具材料支持与本地即时配送，将环保意识转化为可参与的再造实践。用户在线获取知识、购买30分钟送达的DIY工具箱，并可将再造家具重新带回交易系统——将学习、制作与再流通连接起来。

DIY Box 30minAwareness → ActionLower BarrierLoop Back to Trade
04
Shared Capacity Delivery
共享运力配送服务
Integrating idle return-trip capacity with scattered user transport needs — using carpooling-style delivery and shared logistics to reduce second-hand furniture transport costs and improve capacity utilisation.

The main barrier to cross-region used furniture circulation is often not the transaction itself but high logistics cost and inefficient capacity. Cost ratio: moving truck vs express delivery = 1:12 per piece. After delivering goods, truck drivers return empty — idle capacity that can be redistributed. Users post a truck-sharing route, wait for others to join, and link to idle drivers.

二手家具跨区域流通的主要障碍并不总在交易本身，而在高昂的运输成本与低效运力利用。搬家车vs快递：成本比约1:12每件。送货后司机空车返程——这些闲置资源可被重新分配。用户发布拼车路线、等待他人加入、连接闲置司机。

Reduce Long-distance CostImprove Utilisation1:12 Cost Ratio
Deliver / 交付 — Concept & Prototype
CONCEPT &
Prototype
概念方案与原型
Scenario Two: When people want to throw away their old furniture. The concept centres on a three-dimensional lifting-and-transversal furniture storage box — drawing from parking tower logic to enable high-density, damage-free community storage. / 场景二：当人们想处理旧家具时。概念核心是一个升降横移家具存储箱——借鉴立体停车楼逻辑，实现高密度、无损伤的社区集中储存。
Main Source of Inspiration
The Three-Dimensional Garage
Inspired by the vehicle transporter and the three-dimensional garage — sharing the characteristics of transporting, mass storage, and avoiding damage to the furniture.
灵感来源立体停车库，与车辆运输机共享批量存储与防损坏的特性。
Research: Technical from a Patent
Volume Estimation via Infrared
Object → infrared dot matrix → shielding unit obtained → cross-sectional area calculated → volume estimated. Enables digital control of each recycling box to improve efficiency.
红外点阵→遮蔽单元→截面积→体积估算，实现每个存储箱的数字化管理。
Why Use the Storage Box?
Convenient · Efficient · Eco-Friendly
Residents can quickly dispose of furniture
Waste furniture will not pollute the community
Staff can check furniture info in the box anytime
Improves truck utilisation, reduces manual scheduling
Deliver / 交付 — App Touchpoints
USER-FACING
Touchpoints
用户前台触点
High-fidelity screens across two core scenarios — furniture disposal and second-hand matching. Each numbered touchpoint corresponds to a designed interaction in the service journey. / 两个核心场景下的高保真界面：旧家具处置与二手家具匹配，每个编号触点均对应服务旅程中的一个交互设计节点。
01
Scenario — Community Storage Box / 社区存储箱
When People Want to Throw Away Their Old Furniture
01
Find the Box
↓
Opens app and locates the nearest community storage box on the map
打开APP，在地图上找到最近存储箱
02
Submit Info
↓
Upload photo, dimensions, and condition of the furniture
上传家具照片、尺寸与状态信息
03
Navigate
↓
Navigate to the box or request a home pickup service
导航前往或预约上门取件
04
Scan & Confirm
↓
Scan the QR code on the box to confirm the furniture handoff
扫描存储箱二维码，确认家具交接
05
Done
↓
Confirmation screen — old furniture has been successfully handed off
确认提示：旧家具已成功处置
02
Scenario — Furniture Matching & Shared Delivery / 家具匹配与共享运输
When People Want to Buy Second-Hand Furniture
01
Browse & Match
↓
Find second-hand furniture with smart matching recommendations based on style and space
浏览列表，根据风格与空间获取智能匹配推荐
02
Cart & Compare
↓
Review old furniture to sell alongside new furniture to buy — compare combined cost
查看旧家具出售与新家具购入，合并结算对比费用
03
Choose Wait Time
↓
Select a shipping waiting window — longer wait = lower shared delivery cost
选择等待时间窗口，等待越久运费折扣越大
04
Create Truck
↓
Post your route, dates, and furniture — other users with the same route can join
发布路线、日期与家具信息，相同路线用户可加入拼车
05
Confirmed
↓
Delivery confirmed — truck linked to driver, all participants notified
配送确认，货车与司机对接，所有参与用户收到通知
Deliver / 交付 — Service Architecture
HOW THE SERVICE
Works as a System
服务如何作为系统运转
The service blueprint shows the user-facing journey across awareness, trade, disposal, and reuse — while the system map explains how platforms, shops, communities, government actors, and recycling partners support the service behind the scenes. / 服务蓝图展示了用户从认知、交易、处置到再利用的关键路径，系统地图则解释了平台、门店、社区、政府及回收合作方如何在后台协同支撑服务运行。
Service Blueprint / 服务蓝图 — Physical Evidence · User Actions · On Stage · Backstage · Stakeholders to Support
System Map / 系统地图 — The Furniture Party Ecosystem
Deliver / 交付 — Testing & Iteration
TESTING &
Iterations
测试与迭代
Testing the DIY service with a real DIY designer and simulating the full service flow from a consumer's perspective — validating usability and identifying gaps in communication support, content clarity, and professional assistance. / 与真实DIY设计师共同测试DIY服务流程，并从消费者视角模拟完整服务流程，验证可用性并识别沟通支持、内容表达与专业协助方面的问题。
Testing with a DIY Designer / 与DIY设计师测试

"The overall process is smooth. There are many times when communication is needed, and it seems that communication is not possible. The video and text are repeated."

整体流程顺畅。有很多时候需要沟通，但感觉无法沟通。视频和文字内容有重复。

— DIY Designer, Test Participant
Simulating Service Flow / 模拟服务流程

"Sometimes I need professional help in the process. The rest of the process went pretty well."

有时在过程中需要专业帮助。其余流程进行得相当顺利。

— Consumer simulation testing
Iteration Based on Feedback / 基于反馈的迭代
  • Community Chat

    Added in-app community chat — allows fellow DIYers to learn together, discuss progress, and share knowledge. Solves the missing peer-to-peer communication channel identified in testing.

    增加应用内社区聊天——让DIY爱好者共同学习、讨论进度、分享知识，解决测试中发现的点对点沟通缺失问题。
  • Layered Content

    Pictures and text can now be viewed selectively — added teacher presentations alongside videos, reducing repetition. Progressive structure lets users control engagement depth without information overload.

    图文现可选择性查看——在视频旁增加教师讲解，减少内容重复。分层结构让用户自主控制参与深度，避免信息过载。
  • 1-on-1 Expert Sessions

    For DIYers needing immediate professional help, 1-on-1 sessions match the right expert based on project type. This also allows Furnipar to collect customer data to fuel more relevant future workshop content.

    针对需要即时专业帮助的DIY用户，一对一会话根据项目类型匹配合适专家，同时帮助家具派收集客户数据，优化未来工作坊内容的相关性。

Reflection / 项目反思
"Meaningful change in circular systems often depends not on one solution, but on how multiple actors, infrastructures, and motivations are reconnected."
在循环系统中，真正推动改变的往往不是某一个单独的解决方案，而是多方角色、基础设施与行为动机能否被重新连接起来。
— Deng Jin, Project Reflection
What I Learned / 我的收获
FROM PRODUCTS
TO Systems
从产品思维到系统思维

This project helped me move from thinking about products to thinking about systems. Rather than designing a single object, I learned to organise relationships between stakeholders, touchpoints, resources, and service flows.

这个项目让我从"设计一个产品"的思维，逐渐转向"设计一个系统"的思维。相比定义单一物件，我开始更多地组织利益相关者、触点、资源与服务流程之间的关系。

It also showed me that meaningful change in circular systems often depends not on one solution, but on how multiple actors, infrastructures, and motivations are connected.

它也让我意识到，在循环系统中，真正推动改变的往往不是某一个单独的解决方案，而是多方角色、基础设施与行为动机能否被重新连接起来。

Learned 01 — System over Product

Design is not about 'indulging' in one's own experience and making an ideal design. It is about being user-centred, co-created, sequential, evidence-based and holistic — this is service design thinking.

设计不是沉溺于自己的体验并创造理想设计。它需要以用户为中心、共同创造、循序渐进、基于证据、整体考量——这就是服务设计思维。

Learned 02 — Scenario over Object

The designer's role shifts from designing a product to designing a scenario — one that facilitates dialogue between producers and consumers of a service at the same time, rather than optimising for end users alone.

设计师的角色从设计产品转变为设计场景——同时促进服务生产者与消费者之间的对话，而非仅仅优化终端用户体验。

Learned 03 — New Questions

This project raised new questions about three-dimensional space and immersive experience — if design can go beyond service flows into spatial environments, what becomes possible? These questions led directly to the next project.

这个项目引发了关于三维空间与沉浸式体验的新问题——如果设计能超越服务流程进入空间环境，会有什么可能？这些问题直接引导了下一个项目：谨思书局。

Next Project / 下一个项目
